metadata.description: Authoritative references for clean code standards (CC-*), code review practices, security-by-design, observability, refactoring, testing strategy, reliability, and modern linting/SAST tooling.

code_review_practices.name: Google Engineering Practices - What to look for in a code review
code_review_practices.relevance: Durable reviewer focus areas: complexity, tests, and relying on style guides instead of preference debates.

code_review_practices.name: Google Engineering Practices - Small CLs
code_review_practices.relevance: Explains why small, reviewable changes improve review quality and reduce rework.

code_review_practices.name: Google Engineering Practices - Writing good CL descriptions
code_review_practices.relevance: Defines durable expectations for "what" and "why" so future readers can understand intent.

code_review_practices.name: Google Engineering Practices - Code review speed
code_review_practices.relevance: Sets expectations for review responsiveness to reduce process latency without sacrificing quality.

commenting_and_feedback.name: Conventional Comments
commenting_and_feedback.relevance: A lightweight, machine-parseable standard for review comment intent (issue/suggestion/question/nitpick).

security.name: OWASP Top 10
security.relevance: Baseline web application security risks to consider in both coding and review standards.

security.name: NIST SSDF (SP 800-218)
security.relevance: Secure software development framework for governance, practices, and organizational adoption.

observability.name: OpenTelemetry Documentation
observability.relevance: Industry-standard approach to traces, metrics, and logs for observable systems.

normative_language.name: RFC 2119 - Key words for use in RFCs to Indicate Requirement Levels
normative_language.relevance: Defines MUST/SHOULD/MAY requirement wording for stable standards documents.

github_workflow_controls.name: GitHub Docs - Creating a pull request template
github_workflow_controls.relevance: Repository-level PR templates improve review context consistency and reduce back-and-forth.

github_workflow_controls.name: GitHub Docs - About CODEOWNERS
github_workflow_controls.relevance: Routes reviews to responsible owners and helps scale review quality across teams.

github_workflow_controls.name: GitHub Docs - About protected branches
github_workflow_controls.relevance: Explains branch protection controls (required reviews, status checks) for enforcement in CI.

refactoring_and_design.name: Refactoring (Martin Fowler) - Catalog
refactoring_and_design.relevance: Canonical refactoring patterns with intent and mechanics; useful for proposing precise, low-risk refactors.

refactoring_and_design.name: The Twelve-Factor App
refactoring_and_design.relevance: Operational principles for cloud-native apps (config, logs, concurrency, disposability) that inform reusable templates and defaults.

refactoring_and_design.name: C4 Model
refactoring_and_design.relevance: A lightweight diagramming model for communicating architecture with stable levels of detail; useful for skill resources and templates.

refactoring_and_design.name: Documenting Architecture Decisions (Michael Nygard)
refactoring_and_design.relevance: Canonical ADR format and rationale; useful when skills recommend decision records for durability and traceability.

refactoring_and_design.name: AWS Builders' Library
refactoring_and_design.relevance: Operationally grounded essays on reliability and maintainability; good defaults for resilience and scaling patterns in templates.

foundational_books.name: Clean Code (Robert C. Martin)
foundational_books.relevance: Foundational readability and maintainability heuristics; use to inform checklists and examples (avoid copying prose into standards).

foundational_books.name: Code Complete, 2nd Edition (Steve McConnell)
foundational_books.relevance: Construction practices: defensive coding, complexity control, and practical discipline that maps well to CC-* review feedback.

foundational_books.name: The Pragmatic Programmer (20th Anniversary Edition)
foundational_books.relevance: Durable engineering habits: refactoring, testing, automation, and designing for change; good background for skill guidance.

foundational_books.name: Working Effectively with Legacy Code (Michael Feathers)
foundational_books.relevance: Change-safe techniques for legacy systems (seams, characterization tests, risk control); complements refactoring checklists.

testing_strategy.name: Google Testing Blog
testing_strategy.relevance: Testing strategy and maintainability practices from Google engineers; useful background for cross-skill testing guidance.

testing_strategy.name: Playwright Documentation
testing_strategy.relevance: E2E testing for web apps with reliable tooling and cross-browser support; good reference for test automation templates.

testing_strategy.name: Testing Library - Guiding Principles
testing_strategy.relevance: Principles for writing tests that resemble user behavior and avoid brittle implementation-coupled assertions.

observability_and_reliability.name: Google SRE Book
observability_and_reliability.relevance: SLIs/SLOs, incident response, and reliability fundamentals; good baseline for operational and observability guidance.

observability_and_reliability.name: OpenTelemetry Specifications
observability_and_reliability.relevance: Semantic conventions and protocol-level specs for consistent observability across vendors and stacks.

specs_and_standards.name: RFC 9457 - Problem Details for HTTP APIs
specs_and_standards.relevance: Standard error response format for HTTP APIs (updates RFC 7807); helps keep API templates consistent.

specs_and_standards.name: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series
specs_and_standards.relevance: Practical security guidance (auth, session management, headers, input validation) for templates and reviews.

optional_ai_automation.name: TypeScript Native Previews
optional_ai_automation.relevance: Optional: TypeScript native tooling progress; useful when skills discuss build performance and editor/tooling ergonomics.

optional_ai_automation.name: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
optional_ai_automation.relevance: Optional: threat taxonomy for LLM applications (prompt injection, data leakage, model supply chain) for AI-enabled product work.

optional_ai_automation.name: AI Code Review Tools 2026 (Qodo)
optional_ai_automation.relevance: AI-assisted review tools landscape; addresses review throughput bottleneck as AI-generated code volume increases.

optional_ai_automation.name: AI SAST Tools Guide 2026 (Arnica)
optional_ai_automation.relevance: AI-native SAST for faster, more accurate vulnerability detection; covers Cycode, Snyk Code, Semgrep AI capabilities.

modern_linting_tooling.name: Biome vs ESLint Comparison (Better Stack)
modern_linting_tooling.relevance: Modern linter performance comparison; Biome is 10-25x faster than ESLint+Prettier with unified tooling.

modern_linting_tooling.name: Oxlint - Rust-based JavaScript Linter
modern_linting_tooling.relevance: 50-100x faster than ESLint with zero config; production-ready standalone linter from Oxc project.

modern_linting_tooling.name: Ruff - Python Linter and Formatter
modern_linting_tooling.relevance: Rust-based Python linter 10-100x faster than Flake8; drop-in replacement with unified linting and formatting.

modern_linting_tooling.name: Code Quality Metrics for Large Orgs 2026 (Qodo)
modern_linting_tooling.relevance: Technical debt metrics (TDR, code churn, defect ratio), benchmarks, and AI-generated code validation costs.
